title: Lenovo Legion Go 2 Price Explodes to $1,350 Amid RAM Shortage Crisis 'RAMageddon' Hits Gaming Hardware
body:
The gaming world has been dealt a significant blow with the sudden price hike of the Lenovo Legion Go 2, a highly-anticipated handheld gaming console. The device, which was initially expected to be a budget-friendly alternative to traditional gaming setups, has seen its prices more than double in a matter of weeks. The base model, which features a Ryzen Z2 processor, is now listed at $1,499, while the higher-end Z2 Extreme model has skyrocketed to a staggering $1,999. This price jump is not only unexpected but also raises concerns about the affordability of gaming hardware in the market.

The Lenovo Legion Go 2 was initially positioned as a mid-range handheld console that would offer a portable gaming experience without breaking the bank. However, the price hike has now positioned it alongside high-end gaming laptops and consoles. To put this into perspective, the device now costs twice as much as the Xbox Series S, a console that is widely regarded as a high-end gaming device. This price mismatch has left many gamers wondering if the Lenovo Legion Go 2 is still worth the investment, especially when compared to other gaming options available in the market.

One of the key drivers of the price hike is the ongoing shortage of RAM, a crucial component in gaming hardware. The industry-wide shortage, dubbed "RAMageddon," has led to a surge in prices of gaming hardware, making it increasingly difficult for manufacturers to keep up with demand. The Lenovo Legion Go 2, which relies heavily on RAM for its performance, has not been immune to this trend. As a result, the prices of the device have been forced to increase, making it less competitive in the market.

The price hike of the Lenovo Legion Go 2 also raises questions about the viability of gaming handheld consoles as a viable gaming option. While the device offers a unique portable gaming experience, its high price point may deter many gamers who are looking for a more affordable option. In a market where gaming PCs and consoles are becoming increasingly affordable, the Lenovo Legion Go 2 may struggle to find its footing.

In the short term, the price hike of the Lenovo Legion Go 2 is likely to have a significant impact on the gaming industry. As gamers look for alternative options, manufacturers may need to reassess their pricing strategies to remain competitive. However, in the long term, the industry may need to adapt to changing market conditions and find ways to make gaming hardware more affordable for consumers.

As the gaming industry continues to evolve, it will be interesting to see how manufacturers respond to the challenges posed by RAMageddon. Will they find ways to reduce costs and make gaming hardware more affordable, or will the prices continue to rise? One thing is certain - the gaming world will be watching closely to see how this plays out.
